  [acti-010] IF the user requests an exchange/modify AND the user has specified constraints for the replacement (e.g., same difficulty level, specific theme, exact color/size, or other attribute requirements) THEN: (1) call get_product_details(product_id) to inspect candidate replacement variants; (2) verify that the chosen replacement variant(s) exactly satisfy the user's stated constraints before passing new_item_ids to the exchange/modify tool; (3) IF no available variant fully meets the constraints, inform the user, present the closest available alternatives that do meet as many constraints as possible, and ask the user to confirm which alternative to use; (4) IF the user confirms a replacement that does NOT meet one or more previously stated constraints, explicitly restate the mismatched attributes and require an explicit confirmation to proceed.
  [acti-011] IF the user requests exchanging or swapping items between different orders (for example, exchanging a delivered item for an item that currently appears in a different pending order) THEN: (1) verify with get_order_details that the source item_id exists in the delivered order and the target item_id exists in the referenced pending order before taking any action; (2) do NOT attempt to call modify_pending_order_items using item_ids that belong to a different order (prec-007); (3) process the delivered-item side as a return or exchange using the delivered-order tools (return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items) and separately handle the pending-order side (modify_pending_order_items, cancel_pending_order, or re-order) depending on system capabilities and user preference; (4) clearly explain to the user the multi-step nature of the operation (returns/exchanges on one order and modifications/cancellations on the other) and obtain explicit confirmation before proceeding.
  [acti-012] acti-012: IF the user requests multiple exchanges/returns/modifications that span multiple items and/or multiple orders THEN: (1) for each requested exchange/return/modify, call get_order_details and get_product_details as needed to determine each item's original price and the replacement variant price (verifying availability per prec-005 and acti-005); (2) calculate each change's price difference (positive = charge, negative = refund) and then compute the net settlement amount across all changes (sum of individual differences); (3) present the exact per-item differences and the final net amount to the user and request explicit confirmation before executing any action; (4) IF confirmed, include the appropriate payment_method_id(s) when invoking action tools (per prec-006 and prec-004 rules regarding refunds) and execute the required action calls per order (treat each order independently using exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, or cancel_pending_order as appropriate and following acti-011 when multiple orders are involved); (5) after executing, inform the user of each completed change and the net charge or refund that was processed. NOTE: apply this same flow for bulk modifications of pending orders (for example 'update all my pending t-shirt orders'), ensuring the system searches all recent orders to find matching pending items and includes each in the per-item/net calculation and confirmation step.
  [acti-013] IF an action tool call fails with an error that indicates the chosen tool is incompatible with the order's status (for example: 'Non-delivered order cannot be exchanged') THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) to verify the current order status; (2) automatically retry the operation using the correct tool for that status (e.g., if order.status == 'pending' use modify_pending_order_items or modify_pending_order_address/cancel_pending_order as appropriate; if order.status == 'delivered' use exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items); (3) ensure preconditions (availability, payment_method_id, item membership in order) are met before retrying; (4) detect if the failure was caused by a prior action that changed the order (for example, a recently initiated return changed status to 'return requested') and, if so, inform the user that a previous operation altered the order status and explain the implications (e.g., that exchanges may no longer be allowed) and present options (perform exchanges first, proceed with current return and escalate, or undo/resolve with human assistance); (5) if automatic correction would change the user's previously stated instruction or requires confirmation (for example switching from an exchange to a pending-order modification), inform the user of the corrected action and request explicit confirmation before proceeding; (6) if automatic correction cannot resolve the error, treat it as an unrecoverable error and follow escalation per conf-006 (transfer_to_human_agents with summary and error details).
  [acti-014] acti-014: IF an exchange/modify/cancellation action fails (or cannot be executed) because the selected payment method (for example a gift card) has insufficient funds to cover required charges or price differences THEN: (1) calculate the exact shortfall after applying the available balance on the chosen payment source and present the per-item and total shortfall to the user; (2) Offer to charge the remaining shortfall to another existing payment method on the account (listing available saved methods) and require explicit user confirmation before proceeding; (3) IF no other saved payment method exists or the user prefers to use a different card, instruct the user to add the new payment method via the secure payment portal (do NOT request raw card data in chat) and offer to proceed once the user confirms the new method is saved; (4) After the user confirms which payment method(s) to use, retry the action including the appropriate payment_method_id(s) and document the final charges/refunds; (5) If the system does not support split payments across multiple sources, inform the user and offer alternatives (adjust replacement choices, cancel the action, or add sufficient funds to the gift card) before retrying.
  [acti-015] IF the user requests multiple operations on the same order that include both returns and exchanges (or other actions where initiating one will change the order.status) THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) to inspect the current status and the set of items involved; (2) inform the user that initiating a return may change the order status (for example to 'return requested') and that this status change can prevent subsequent exchanges or other modifications on that same order; (3) recommend performing exchanges or modifications first and then initiating returns where possible to avoid tool incompatibility; (4) prompt the user to confirm the preferred order of operations (e.g., "Do you want me to do the exchanges first, then the returns, or start the return now?"); (5) proceed according to the user's explicit preference; (6) if the user prefers to start the return immediately, proceed but be prepared to handle and surface any subsequent action-tool errors and follow acti-013 escalation/repair flows if needed.

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF the user has not given identification THEN ask for either name+zip OR email before proceeding
  [conf-002] IF multiple product options exist (e.g., backlight, color, compatibility) THEN ask clarifying questions about those specific options before making a selection
  [conf-003] IF the user states final choices for exchanges/modifications/returns/cancellations THEN: - IF the user also issues an explicit instruction to proceed (e.g., 'please do this', 'go ahead and replace it with X', 'start the return') treat that as confirmation and proceed with the appropriate action tool call after restating the exact changes, affected order_id(s), item_id(s), and the refund/charge amounts and payment destinations. - OTHERWISE, restate the exact changes (items being replaced/removed/returned/canceled, selected replacement variants, order_id(s) involved, and price differences or refund amounts) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing the change. BEFORE executing any return or cancellation action, verify via get_order_details that each referenced item_id belongs to the target order_id and confirm that order_id with the user; DO NOT call a return/cancel tool if an item_id is not present in the target order—first resolve the correct order or item identification with the user.
  [conf-004] IF the user requests a refund to a non-original payment method THEN inform the user that refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card
  [conf-005] AFTER executing a cancel/modify/exchange/return tool call THEN inform the user that the change is complete and ask if anything else is needed
  [conf-006] IF the user requests to be connected to a human agent or becomes escalated and requests human assistance THEN call transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) to escalate and include a brief summary of the issue and recent actions. ALSO, IF after following the playbook's precondition checks and verification steps an automated action or modification fails with unrecoverable system errors (for example: 'Variant not found', 'Non-pending order cannot be modified', or other persistent tool errors that cannot be resolved by user clarification), escalate to a human agent via transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) and include the error details, steps already taken, and the relevant order/user identifiers.
  [conf-007] IF the user requests cancellation of a single item from a pending order THEN check whether the system supports cancelling that item alone; IF single-item cancellation is not supported (and cancelling will cancel the entire order) THEN inform the user that cancelling the item would require cancelling the whole order and ask for explicit confirmation before invoking cancel_pending_order. ALSO, if single-item cancellation is not supported, offer the user the option to cancel the entire order and immediately place a new order for the items they want to keep (i.e., cancel-and-reorder), and require explicit confirmation before proceeding with cancel_pending_order.
  [conf-008] IF the user requests that refunds or exchange price adjustments be directed to a different payment method AND the refund/adjustment has already been processed by the system, THEN inform the user that processed refunds cannot be redirected, state which payment method received the refund, and offer available remedies (e.g., issue a separate refund/credit in the requested method if policy permits, provide guidance to contact the payment provider for next steps, or escalate to a human agent for review). If the refund has not yet been executed, confirm the requested payment_method_id explicitly and update the action to use that payment method before calling the action tool.
  [conf-009] IF a user requests expedited processing of a refund (or asks whether a refund can be made faster than the standard timeline) THEN: (1) inform the user of the typical processing timeline (e.g., refunds typically take 5-7 business days) and whether the current refund has already been initiated; (2) offer to escalate the request to a human agent for review if the user needs the refund faster or requests an exception, calling transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) and including order_id, refund details, payment method, and the user's request for expedited handling; and (3) if escalation is requested, obtain the user's explicit consent to escalate and proceed to transfer_to_human_agents with the summary.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF identification (name+zip OR email) has not been collected THEN do not perform user lookups or actions that require account-wide access. EXCEPTION: if the user provides a valid order_id for a specific order, it is permissible to call get_order_details(order_id) and to perform order-scoped inquiries or actions for that order (e.g., check status, provide tracking, show order total, or initiate order-specific returns/exchanges) without prior account identification. Do not use the order_id to perform broader user lookups or act on other orders without collecting identification.
  [prec-002] IF an order's status is 'pending' THEN use modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order for changes/cancellation; do not use delivered-order tools on pending orders
  [prec-003] IF an order's status is 'delivered' THEN use exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items for exchanges/returns; do not use pending-order tools on delivered orders
  [prec-004] IF the user requests a refund to a payment method other than the original payment method THEN: - FIRST, inspect the order's payment_history (via get_order_details) to determine the original payment method before attempting any refund/return tool call. - Refunds must be issued to the original payment method used for the order or to an existing gift card associated with the account. If the user requests an alternate payment method, inform them that the system requires refunds to go to the original payment method (or an existing gift card) and DO NOT call the action tool with a non-original payment_method_id. - IF the original payment method for the order is a gift card THEN do not perform the refund to an alternate non-gift-card method and inform the user of the policy; offer to proceed with refund to the original/another gift card. - AFTER the refund has been processed, follow conf-008 for redirection or escalation guidance.
  [prec-005] IF a replacement variant chosen is not available (available != true) THEN do not pass that variant as new_item_id and instead inform the user and offer alternative available variants
  [prec-006] IF calling any action tool that requires payment information THEN include a payment_method_id (typically the original payment method) when invoking the tool
  [prec-007] IF item_ids to be acted upon are not present in the target order's items list THEN do not perform the action on that order
  [prec-008] prec-008: IF the user requests a refund to a gift card (or requests cancellation with refund directed to a gift card) THEN: (1) verify the user has an existing eligible gift card on their account (via get_user_details or payment methods inspection); (2) obtain the gift_card payment_method_id or identifier and confirm with the user which gift card should receive the refund; (3) include that payment_method_id when invoking cancel_pending_order or other refund/cancellation tools. IF no gift card exists, inform the user and offer the available alternatives (refund to original payment method or issue a new gift card/credit if policy allows) before calling the action tool.
  [prec-009] prec-009: IF a replacement new_item_id equals the original item_id being replaced (i.e., identical item_id) THEN do not call exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items with that identical mapping. Instead inform the user that the selected replacement is identical to the original and request a different replacement or explicitly confirm that they meant to request a replacement (for example, choosing a different variant or requesting a replacement of a used/damaged unit).
  [prec-010] prec-010: DO NOT request, collect, or accept full raw payment card details (card number, CVV, expiry) or other sensitive payment credentials in chat. If a user attempts to provide such details, immediately inform them that for security reasons you cannot process or store that information, instruct them to add the payment method through the service's secure payment portal or app, and provide step-by-step guidance for doing so. If a payment is required to complete an action, offer to (a) charge an existing saved payment method on the account (with explicit user confirmation) or (b) wait for the user to add a new method via the secure portal before proceeding.

IF get_order_details(order_id) returns 'Order not found' THEN: (1) automatically retry the lookup by normalizing the order_id — trim whitespace, adjust case, and try with and without a leading '#' — before asking the user to re-enter it; (2) if normalization still fails and the user has already provided or subsequently provides account identification (name+zip or email), automatically retrieve get_user_details(user_id) and enumerate the user's recent orders (calling get_order_details for each) to help locate the intended order; (3) if no account identification is available, prompt the user to provide name+zip OR email OR to check the order confirmation email for the correct ID.

IF find_user_id_by_email(email) returns 'User not found' THEN: (1) inform the user that the email lookup failed; (2) immediately offer (and request) alternative identification methods (name+zip or an order_id) and, if provided, attempt find_user_id_by_name_zip or direct order lookup; and (3) do not treat the email lookup failure as final without prompting alternatives.

IF an action tool call fails with an error that indicates the chosen tool is incompatible with the order's status (for example: 'Non-delivered order cannot be exchanged') THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) to verify the current order status; (2) automatically retry the operation using the correct tool for that status (e.g., if order.status == 'pending' use modify_pending_order_items or modify_pending_order_address/cancel_pending_order as appropriate; if order.status == 'delivered' use exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items); (3) ensure preconditions (availability, payment_method_id, item membership in order) are met before retrying; (4) detect if the failure was caused by a prior action that changed the order (for example, a recently initiated return changed status to 'return requested') and, if so, inform the user that a previous operation altered the order status and explain the implications (e.g., that exchanges may no longer be allowed) and present options (perform exchanges first, proceed with current return and escalate, or undo/resolve with human assistance); (5) if automatic correction would change the user's previously stated instruction or requires confirmation (for example switching from an exchange to a pending-order modification), inform the user of the corrected action and request explicit confirmation before proceeding; (6) if automatic correction cannot resolve the error, treat it as an unrecoverable error and follow escalation per conf-006 (transfer_to_human_agents with summary and error details).

IF a user requests expedited processing of a refund (or asks whether a refund can be made faster than the standard timeline) THEN: (1) inform the user of the typical processing timeline (e.g., refunds typically take 5-7 business days) and whether the current refund has already been initiated; (2) offer to escalate the request to a human agent for review if the user needs the refund faster or requests an exception, calling transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) and including order_id, refund details, payment method, and the user's request for expedited handling; and (3) if escalation is requested, obtain the user's explicit consent to escalate and proceed to transfer_to_human_agents with the summary.

prec-010: DO NOT request, collect, or accept full raw payment card details (card number, CVV, expiry) or other sensitive payment credentials in chat. If a user attempts to provide such details, immediately inform them that for security reasons you cannot process or store that information, instruct them to add the payment method through the service's secure payment portal or app, and provide step-by-step guidance for doing so. If a payment is required to complete an action, offer to (a) charge an existing saved payment method on the account (with explicit user confirmation) or (b) wait for the user to add a new method via the secure portal before proceeding.

acti-014: IF an exchange/modify/cancellation action fails (or cannot be executed) because the selected payment method (for example a gift card) has insufficient funds to cover required charges or price differences THEN: (1) calculate the exact shortfall after applying the available balance on the chosen payment source and present the per-item and total shortfall to the user; (2) Offer to charge the remaining shortfall to another existing payment method on the account (listing available saved methods) and require explicit user confirmation before proceeding; (3) IF no other saved payment method exists or the user prefers to use a different card, instruct the user to add the new payment method via the secure payment portal (do NOT request raw card data in chat) and offer to proceed once the user confirms the new method is saved; (4) After the user confirms which payment method(s) to use, retry the action including the appropriate payment_method_id(s) and document the final charges/refunds; (5) If the system does not support split payments across multiple sources, inform the user and offer alternatives (adjust replacement choices, cancel the action, or add sufficient funds to the gift card) before retrying.

IF the user provides a user_id (for example their account handle or internal id) THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the account profile, saved addresses, payment methods, and recent orders before proceeding with order-scoped or account-scoped actions.

IF the user requests multiple operations on the same order that include both returns and exchanges (or other actions where initiating one will change the order.status) THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) to inspect the current status and the set of items involved; (2) inform the user that initiating a return may change the order status (for example to 'return requested') and that this status change can prevent subsequent exchanges or other modifications on that same order; (3) recommend performing exchanges or modifications first and then initiating returns where possible to avoid tool incompatibility; (4) prompt the user to confirm the preferred order of operations (e.g., "Do you want me to do the exchanges first, then the returns, or start the return now?"); (5) proceed according to the user's explicit preference; (6) if the user prefers to start the return immediately, proceed but be prepared to handle and surface any subsequent action-tool errors and follow acti-013 escalation/repair flows if needed.

iterations.policy:
## Information Gathering
  [info-001] IF the user provides name and zip THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) and then call get_user_details(user_id)
  [info-002] IF the user provides an email THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) and then call get_user_details(user_id)
  [info-003] IF the user requests any action or inquiry about an order THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for the relevant order(s) before taking action
  [info-004] IF the user asks about available product variants or options THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to inspect variants and availability
  [info-005] IF the user asks broadly what product categories exist THEN call list_all_product_types()
  [info-006] IF the user asks to locate an item or identify which order contains an item but does not provide an order ID (or cannot recall it) THEN retrieve get_order_details for each of the user's recent orders to search for matching items/orders before asking additional clarifying questions or taking action.
  [info-007] IF the user asks for the number of available variants/options for a product (e.g., 'how many t-shirt options are available?') THEN: (1) IF product_id is not known, call list_all_product_types() to locate the product_id for that category; (2) call get_product_details(product_id); (3) count and report variants where available=true (i.e., only currently available options); and (4) present the exact count and offer to list/filter those available variants or proceed with selection/exchange.
  [info-008] IF get_order_details(order_id) returns 'Order not found' THEN: (1) automatically retry the lookup by normalizing the order_id — trim whitespace, adjust case, and try with and without a leading '#' — before asking the user to re-enter it; (2) if normalization still fails and the user has already provided or subsequently provides account identification (name+zip or email), automatically retrieve get_user_details(user_id) and enumerate the user's recent orders (calling get_order_details for each) to help locate the intended order; (3) if no account identification is available, prompt the user to provide name+zip OR email OR to check the order confirmation email for the correct ID.
  [info-009] IF find_user_id_by_email(email) returns 'User not found' THEN: (1) inform the user that the email lookup failed; (2) immediately offer (and request) alternative identification methods (name+zip or an order_id) and, if provided, attempt find_user_id_by_name_zip or direct order lookup; and (3) do not treat the email lookup failure as final without prompting alternatives.
  [info-011] IF the user provides a user_id (for example their account handle or internal id) THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the account profile, saved addresses, payment methods, and recent orders before proceeding with order-scoped or account-scoped actions.

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF the user requests exchanging items from a delivered order AND the user has confirmed the replacement choices THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). BEFORE calling exchange_delivered_order_items: (1) verify each new_item_id is available=true by calling get_product_details(product_id) for the relevant product(s); (2) ensure each new_item_id is different from the corresponding original item_id (the system will reject replacements identical to the original); (3) confirm that all new_item_ids exist and are distinct from the set of item_ids being replaced. IF any candidate replacement is unavailable or invalid (including being identical to the original), inform the user, present the next available alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the final replacement list. Include payment_method_id when required.
  [acti-002] IF the user requests returning items from a delivered order AND the user has confirmed the refund destination THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF the user requests modifying items in a pending order AND the user has confirmed the replacement choices THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). Note: the modify_pending_order_items tool requires the number of new_item_ids to match the number of item_ids being replaced. BEFORE calling modify_pending_order_items: (1) verify each new_item_id is available=true by calling get_product_details(product_id) for the relevant product(s); (2) ensure each new_item_id is different from the corresponding original item_id (the system will reject replacements identical to the original); and (3) confirm that all new_item_ids exist and are distinct from the set of item_ids being replaced. IF any candidate replacement is unavailable or invalid, inform the user, present the next available alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the final replacement list. IF the user requests removing items from a pending order without providing replacement new_item_ids THEN: (1) check whether the system supports single-item cancellation for that order (see conf-007). (2) DO NOT call modify_pending_order_items with empty new_item_ids. (3) If single-item cancellation is supported, follow that flow; otherwise offer cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) and suggest re-ordering the items the user wants to keep. Include payment_method_id when required.
  [acti-004] IF the user requests cancelling a pending order THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason, payment_method_id?) where supported. Use the user's provided cancellation reason when supplied. If the user does not supply a reason but gives an explicit affirmative instruction to cancel (e.g., 'Just do it'), use a standard default reason such as 'no longer needed' when invoking cancel_pending_order (or first confirm if the user appears uncertain). BEFORE invoking cancel_pending_order: (1) inspect the order's payment_history (via get_order_details) to determine the original payment method; (2) if the user requests the refund to be issued to a gift card, confirm that an eligible gift card exists on the account and specify that gift card's payment_method_id when calling cancel_pending_order; (3) if the user requests a refund to a non-original non-gift-card method, inform the user that refunds must go to the original payment method or an existing gift card and do NOT call cancel_pending_order with a non-original payment_method_id.
  [acti-005] IF selecting a replacement variant for an exchange/modify THEN choose a variant item_id that has available=true from get_product_details before passing new_item_ids to the action tool
  [acti-006] IF multiple orders/locations must be searched for the items the user references THEN retrieve get_order_details for each candidate order before deciding which order to act on
  [acti-007] IF the user requests updating the delivery address for a pending order AND provides or confirms the new address THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
  [acti-008] [acti-008] IF the user requests updating their default (account) address AND provides or confirms the new address THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the stored default address.
  [acti-009] acti-009: IF the user asks to set their default address to an address that already appears on a recent order and declines to re-enter it (e.g., 'use the address from my recent order') THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) for the referenced recent order to retrieve the address; (2) confirm with the user which order/address they mean (explicit confirmation required) and whether they want that address applied only to the referenced pending order or also saved as their account default; (3) IF the user wants to use the address for a pending order, call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip); (4) IF the user also wants to save it as their default, call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) after confirming; (5) do not assume an address appearing on an order is already in the user's stored profile address list — always confirm source and intent before modifying either the order or the account.
  [acti-010] IF the user requests an exchange/modify AND the user has specified constraints for the replacement (e.g., same difficulty level, specific theme, exact color/size, or other attribute requirements) THEN: (1) call get_product_details(product_id) to inspect candidate replacement variants; (2) verify that the chosen replacement variant(s) exactly satisfy the user's stated constraints before passing new_item_ids to the exchange/modify tool; (3) IF no available variant fully meets the constraints, inform the user, present the closest available alternatives that do meet as many constraints as possible, and ask the user to confirm which alternative to use; (4) IF the user confirms a replacement that does NOT meet one or more previously stated constraints, explicitly restate the mismatched attributes and require an explicit confirmation to proceed.
  [acti-011] IF the user requests exchanging or swapping items between different orders (for example, exchanging a delivered item for an item that currently appears in a different pending order) THEN: (1) verify with get_order_details that the source item_id exists in the delivered order and the target item_id exists in the referenced pending order before taking any action; (2) do NOT attempt to call modify_pending_order_items using item_ids that belong to a different order (prec-007); (3) process the delivered-item side as a return or exchange using the delivered-order tools (return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items) and separately handle the pending-order side (modify_pending_order_items, cancel_pending_order, or re-order) depending on system capabilities and user preference; (4) clearly explain to the user the multi-step nature of the operation (returns/exchanges on one order and modifications/cancellations on the other) and obtain explicit confirmation before proceeding.
  [acti-012] acti-012: IF the user requests multiple exchanges/returns/modifications that span multiple items and/or multiple orders THEN: (1) for each requested exchange/return/modify, call get_order_details and get_product_details as needed to determine each item's original price and the replacement variant price (verifying availability per prec-005 and acti-005); (2) calculate each change's price difference (positive = charge, negative = refund) and then compute the net settlement amount across all changes (sum of individual differences); (3) present the exact per-item differences and the final net amount to the user and request explicit confirmation before executing any action; (4) IF confirmed, include the appropriate payment_method_id(s) when invoking action tools (per prec-006 and prec-004 rules regarding refunds) and execute the required action calls per order (treat each order independently using exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, or cancel_pending_order as appropriate and following acti-011 when multiple orders are involved); (5) after executing, inform the user of each completed change and the net charge or refund that was processed. NOTE: apply this same flow for bulk modifications of pending orders (for example 'update all my pending t-shirt orders'), ensuring the system searches all recent orders to find matching pending items and includes each in the per-item/net calculation and confirmation step.
  [acti-013] IF an action tool call fails with an error that indicates the chosen tool is incompatible with the order's status (for example: 'Non-delivered order cannot be exchanged') THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) to verify the current order status; (2) automatically retry the operation using the correct tool for that status (e.g., if order.status == 'pending' use modify_pending_order_items or modify_pending_order_address/cancel_pending_order as appropriate; if order.status == 'delivered' use exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items); (3) ensure preconditions (availability, payment_method_id, item membership in order) are met before retrying; (4) detect if the failure was caused by a prior action that changed the order (for example, a recently initiated return changed status to 'return requested') and, if so, inform the user that a previous operation altered the order status and explain the implications (e.g., that exchanges may no longer be allowed) and present options (perform exchanges first, proceed with current return and escalate, or undo/resolve with human assistance); (5) if automatic correction would change the user's previously stated instruction or requires confirmation (for example switching from an exchange to a pending-order modification), inform the user of the corrected action and request explicit confirmation before proceeding; (6) if automatic correction cannot resolve the error, treat it as an unrecoverable error and follow escalation per conf-006 (transfer_to_human_agents with summary and error details).
  [acti-014] acti-014: IF an exchange/modify/cancellation action fails (or cannot be executed) because the selected payment method (for example a gift card) has insufficient funds to cover required charges or price differences THEN: (1) calculate the exact shortfall after applying the available balance on the chosen payment source and present the per-item and total shortfall to the user; (2) Offer to charge the remaining shortfall to another existing payment method on the account (listing available saved methods) and require explicit user confirmation before proceeding; (3) IF no other saved payment method exists or the user prefers to use a different card, instruct the user to add the new payment method via the secure payment portal (do NOT request raw card data in chat) and offer to proceed once the user confirms the new method is saved; (4) After the user confirms which payment method(s) to use, retry the action including the appropriate payment_method_id(s) and document the final charges/refunds; (5) If the system does not support split payments across multiple sources, inform the user and offer alternatives (adjust replacement choices, cancel the action, or add sufficient funds to the gift card) before retrying.
  [acti-015] IF the user requests multiple operations on the same order that include both returns and exchanges (or other actions where initiating one will change the order.status) THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) to inspect the current status and the set of items involved; (2) inform the user that initiating a return may change the order status (for example to 'return requested') and that this status change can prevent subsequent exchanges or other modifications on that same order; (3) recommend performing exchanges or modifications first and then initiating returns where possible to avoid tool incompatibility; (4) prompt the user to confirm the preferred order of operations (e.g., "Do you want me to do the exchanges first, then the returns, or start the return now?"); (5) proceed according to the user's explicit preference; (6) if the user prefers to start the return immediately, proceed but be prepared to handle and surface any subsequent action-tool errors and follow acti-013 escalation/repair flows if needed.

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF the user has not given identification THEN ask for either name+zip OR email before proceeding
  [conf-002] IF multiple product options exist (e.g., backlight, color, compatibility) THEN ask clarifying questions about those specific options before making a selection
  [conf-003] IF the user states final choices for exchanges/modifications/returns/cancellations THEN: - IF the user also issues an explicit instruction to proceed (e.g., 'please do this', 'go ahead and replace it with X', 'start the return') treat that as confirmation and proceed with the appropriate action tool call after restating the exact changes, affected order_id(s), item_id(s), and the refund/charge amounts and payment destinations. - OTHERWISE, restate the exact changes (items being replaced/removed/returned/canceled, selected replacement variants, order_id(s) involved, and price differences or refund amounts) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing the change. BEFORE executing any return or cancellation action, verify via get_order_details that each referenced item_id belongs to the target order_id and confirm that order_id with the user; DO NOT call a return/cancel tool if an item_id is not present in the target order—first resolve the correct order or item identification with the user.
  [conf-004] IF the user requests a refund to a non-original payment method THEN inform the user that refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card
  [conf-005] AFTER executing a cancel/modify/exchange/return tool call THEN inform the user that the change is complete and ask if anything else is needed
  [conf-006] IF the user requests to be connected to a human agent or becomes escalated and requests human assistance THEN call transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) to escalate and include a brief summary of the issue and recent actions. ALSO, IF after following the playbook's precondition checks and verification steps an automated action or modification fails with unrecoverable system errors (for example: 'Variant not found', 'Non-pending order cannot be modified', or other persistent tool errors that cannot be resolved by user clarification), escalate to a human agent via transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) and include the error details, steps already taken, and the relevant order/user identifiers.
  [conf-007] IF the user requests cancellation of a single item from a pending order THEN check whether the system supports cancelling that item alone; IF single-item cancellation is not supported (and cancelling will cancel the entire order) THEN inform the user that cancelling the item would require cancelling the whole order and ask for explicit confirmation before invoking cancel_pending_order. ALSO, if single-item cancellation is not supported, offer the user the option to cancel the entire order and immediately place a new order for the items they want to keep (i.e., cancel-and-reorder), and require explicit confirmation before proceeding with cancel_pending_order.
  [conf-008] IF the user requests that refunds or exchange price adjustments be directed to a different payment method AND the refund/adjustment has already been processed by the system, THEN inform the user that processed refunds cannot be redirected, state which payment method received the refund, and offer available remedies (e.g., issue a separate refund/credit in the requested method if policy permits, provide guidance to contact the payment provider for next steps, or escalate to a human agent for review). If the refund has not yet been executed, confirm the requested payment_method_id explicitly and update the action to use that payment method before calling the action tool.
  [conf-009] IF a user requests expedited processing of a refund (or asks whether a refund can be made faster than the standard timeline) THEN: (1) inform the user of the typical processing timeline (e.g., refunds typically take 5-7 business days) and whether the current refund has already been initiated; (2) offer to escalate the request to a human agent for review if the user needs the refund faster or requests an exception, calling transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) and including order_id, refund details, payment method, and the user's request for expedited handling; and (3) if escalation is requested, obtain the user's explicit consent to escalate and proceed to transfer_to_human_agents with the summary.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF identification (name+zip OR email) has not been collected THEN do not perform user lookups or actions that require account-wide access. EXCEPTION: if the user provides a valid order_id for a specific order, it is permissible to call get_order_details(order_id) and to perform order-scoped inquiries or actions for that order (e.g., check status, provide tracking, show order total, or initiate order-specific returns/exchanges) without prior account identification. Do not use the order_id to perform broader user lookups or act on other orders without collecting identification.
  [prec-002] IF an order's status is 'pending' THEN use modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order for changes/cancellation; do not use delivered-order tools on pending orders
  [prec-003] IF an order's status is 'delivered' THEN use exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items for exchanges/returns; do not use pending-order tools on delivered orders
  [prec-004] IF the user requests a refund to a payment method other than the original payment method THEN: - FIRST, inspect the order's payment_history (via get_order_details) to determine the original payment method before attempting any refund/return tool call. - Refunds must be issued to the original payment method used for the order or to an existing gift card associated with the account. If the user requests an alternate payment method, inform them that the system requires refunds to go to the original payment method (or an existing gift card) and DO NOT call the action tool with a non-original payment_method_id. - IF the original payment method for the order is a gift card THEN do not perform the refund to an alternate non-gift-card method and inform the user of the policy; offer to proceed with refund to the original/another gift card. - AFTER the refund has been processed, follow conf-008 for redirection or escalation guidance.
  [prec-005] IF a replacement variant chosen is not available (available != true) THEN do not pass that variant as new_item_id and instead inform the user and offer alternative available variants
  [prec-006] IF calling any action tool that requires payment information THEN include a payment_method_id (typically the original payment method) when invoking the tool
  [prec-007] IF item_ids to be acted upon are not present in the target order's items list THEN do not perform the action on that order
  [prec-008] prec-008: IF the user requests a refund to a gift card (or requests cancellation with refund directed to a gift card) THEN: (1) verify the user has an existing eligible gift card on their account (via get_user_details or payment methods inspection); (2) obtain the gift_card payment_method_id or identifier and confirm with the user which gift card should receive the refund; (3) include that payment_method_id when invoking cancel_pending_order or other refund/cancellation tools. IF no gift card exists, inform the user and offer the available alternatives (refund to original payment method or issue a new gift card/credit if policy allows) before calling the action tool.
  [prec-009] prec-009: IF a replacement new_item_id equals the original item_id being replaced (i.e., identical item_id) THEN do not call exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items with that identical mapping. Instead inform the user that the selected replacement is identical to the original and request a different replacement or explicitly confirm that they meant to request a replacement (for example, choosing a different variant or requesting a replacement of a used/damaged unit).
  [prec-010] prec-010: DO NOT request, collect, or accept full raw payment card details (card number, CVV, expiry) or other sensitive payment credentials in chat. If a user attempts to provide such details, immediately inform them that for security reasons you cannot process or store that information, instruct them to add the payment method through the service's secure payment portal or app, and provide step-by-step guidance for doing so. If a payment is required to complete an action, offer to (a) charge an existing saved payment method on the account (with explicit user confirmation) or (b) wait for the user to add a new method via the secure portal before proceeding.

actions.content: IF the user asks for the number of available variants/options for a product (e.g., 'how many t-shirt options are available?') THEN: (1) IF product_id is not known, call list_all_product_types() to locate the product_id for that category; (2) call get_product_details(product_id); (3) count and report variants where available=true (i.e., only currently available options); and (4) present the exact count and offer to list/filter those available variants or proceed with selection/exchange.

IF the user requests an exchange/modify AND the user has specified constraints for the replacement (e.g., same difficulty level, specific theme, exact color/size, or other attribute requirements) THEN: (1) call get_product_details(product_id) to inspect candidate replacement variants; (2) verify that the chosen replacement variant(s) exactly satisfy the user's stated constraints before passing new_item_ids to the exchange/modify tool; (3) IF no available variant fully meets the constraints, inform the user, present the closest available alternatives that do meet as many constraints as possible, and ask the user to confirm which alternative to use; (4) IF the user confirms a replacement that does NOT meet one or more previously stated constraints, explicitly restate the mismatched attributes and require an explicit confirmation to proceed.

IF the user requests that refunds or exchange price adjustments be directed to a different payment method AND the refund/adjustment has already been processed by the system, THEN inform the user that processed refunds cannot be redirected, state which payment method received the refund, and offer available remedies (e.g., issue a separate refund/credit in the requested method if policy permits, provide guidance to contact the payment provider for next steps, or escalate to a human agent for review). If the refund has not yet been executed, confirm the requested payment_method_id explicitly and update the action to use that payment method before calling the action tool.

actions.old_content: IF the user requests exchanging items from a delivered order AND the user has confirmed the replacement choices THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
actions.new_content: IF the user requests exchanging items from a delivered order AND the user has confirmed the replacement choices THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). BEFORE calling exchange_delivered_order_items: (1) verify each new_item_id is available=true by calling get_product_details(product_id) for the relevant product(s); (2) ensure each new_item_id is different from the corresponding original item_id (the system will reject replacements identical to the original); (3) confirm that all new_item_ids exist and are distinct from the set of item_ids being replaced. IF any candidate replacement is unavailable or invalid (including being identical to the original), inform the user, present the next available alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the final replacement list. Include payment_method_id when required.

actions.old_content: IF the user requests cancelling a pending order THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). Use the user's provided cancellation reason when supplied. If the user does not supply a reason but gives an explicit affirmative instruction to cancel (e.g., 'Just do it'), use a standard default reason such as 'no longer needed' when invoking cancel_pending_order (or first confirm if the user appears uncertain).
actions.new_content: IF the user requests cancelling a pending order THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason, payment_method_id?) where supported. Use the user's provided cancellation reason when supplied. If the user does not supply a reason but gives an explicit affirmative instruction to cancel (e.g., 'Just do it'), use a standard default reason such as 'no longer needed' when invoking cancel_pending_order (or first confirm if the user appears uncertain). BEFORE invoking cancel_pending_order: (1) inspect the order's payment_history (via get_order_details) to determine the original payment method; (2) if the user requests the refund to be issued to a gift card, confirm that an eligible gift card exists on the account and specify that gift card's payment_method_id when calling cancel_pending_order; (3) if the user requests a refund to a non-original non-gift-card method, inform the user that refunds must go to the original payment method or an existing gift card and do NOT call cancel_pending_order with a non-original payment_method_id.

IF no gift card exists, inform the user and offer the available alternatives (refund to original payment method or issue a new gift card/credit if policy allows) before calling the action tool.

actions.old_content: IF the user requests to be connected to a human agent or becomes escalated and requests human assistance THEN call transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) to escalate and include a brief summary of the issue and recent actions.
actions.new_content: IF the user requests to be connected to a human agent or becomes escalated and requests human assistance THEN call transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) to escalate and include a brief summary of the issue and recent actions. ALSO, IF after following the playbook's precondition checks and verification steps an automated action or modification fails with unrecoverable system errors (for example: 'Variant not found', 'Non-pending order cannot be modified', or other persistent tool errors that cannot be resolved by user clarification), escalate to a human agent via transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) and include the error details, steps already taken, and the relevant order/user identifiers.

actions.content: IF a get_order_details(order_id) call returns 'Order not found' THEN: (1) automatically retry the lookup by normalizing the order_id (try with and without a leading '#' and trim whitespace/case variations) BEFORE asking the user to re-enter the ID; (2) if normalization still fails and the user has provided account identification (name+zip or email) then use that identification to list the user's recent orders (get_user_details -> iterate get_order_details for each recent order) to locate the intended order; and (3) if no account identification is available, prompt the user to provide name+zip OR email OR to double-check the order confirmation email for the correct ID.

IF find_user_id_by_email(email) returns 'User not found' THEN: (1) inform the user that the email lookup failed; (2) immediately offer (and request) alternative identification methods (name+zip or an order_id) and, if provided, attempt find_user_id_by_name_zip or direct order lookup; and (3) do not treat the email lookup failure as final without prompting alternatives.

IF get_order_details(order_id) returns 'Order not found' but the user has provided or subsequently provides account identification, THEN automatically retrieve get_user_details(user_id) and enumerate the user's recent orders (get_order_details for each) to help the user identify the correct order_id before asking them to re-supply the ID.

IF an action tool call fails with an error that indicates the chosen tool is incompatible with the order's status (for example: 'Non-delivered order cannot be exchanged') THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) to verify the current order status; (2) automatically retry the operation using the correct tool for that status (e.g., if order.status == 'pending' use modify_pending_order_items or modify_pending_order_address/cancel_pending_order as appropriate; if order.status == 'delivered' use exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items); (3) ensure preconditions (availability, payment_method_id, item membership in order) are met before retrying; (4) if automatic correction would change the user's previously stated instruction or requires confirmation (for example switching from an exchange to a pending-order modification), inform the user of the corrected action and request explicit confirmation before proceeding; (5) if automatic correction cannot resolve the error, treat it as an unrecoverable error and follow escalation per conf-006 (transfer_to_human_agents with summary and error details).

IF a user requests expedited processing of a refund (or asks whether a refund can be made faster than the standard timeline) THEN: (1) inform the user of the typical processing timeline (e.g., refunds typically take 5-7 business days) and whether the current refund has already been initiated; (2) offer to escalate the request to a human agent for review if the user needs the refund faster or requests an exception, calling transfer_to_human_agents({summary}) and including order_id, refund details, payment method, and the user's request for expedited handling; and (3) if escalation is requested, obtain the user's explicit consent to escalate and proceed to transfer_to_human_agents with the summary.

actions.old_content: IF the user requests multiple exchanges/returns that span multiple items and/or multiple orders THEN: (1) for each requested exchange/return, call get_order_details and get_product_details as needed to determine each item's original price and the replacement variant price (verifying availability per prec-005 and acti-005); (2) calculate each exchange's price difference (positive = charge, negative = refund) and then compute the net settlement amount across all exchanges (sum of individual differences); (3) present the exact per-item differences and the final net amount to the user and request explicit confirmation before executing any action; (4) IF confirmed, include the appropriate payment_method_id(s) when invoking action tools (per prec-006 and prec-004 rules regarding refunds) and execute the required action calls per order (treat each order independently using exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items/cancel_pending_order as appropriate and following acti-011 when multiple orders are involved); (5) after executing, inform the user of each completed change and the net charge or refund that was processed.
actions.new_content: acti-012: IF the user requests multiple exchanges/returns/modifications that span multiple items and/or multiple orders THEN: (1) for each requested exchange/return/modify, call get_order_details and get_product_details as needed to determine each item's original price and the replacement variant price (verifying availability per prec-005 and acti-005); (2) calculate each change's price difference (positive = charge, negative = refund) and then compute the net settlement amount across all changes (sum of individual differences); (3) present the exact per-item differences and the final net amount to the user and request explicit confirmation before executing any action; (4) IF confirmed, include the appropriate payment_method_id(s) when invoking action tools (per prec-006 and prec-004 rules regarding refunds) and execute the required action calls per order (treat each order independently using exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, or cancel_pending_order as appropriate and following acti-011 when multiple orders are involved); (5) after executing, inform the user of each completed change and the net charge or refund that was processed. NOTE: apply this same flow for bulk modifications of pending orders (for example 'update all my pending t-shirt orders'), ensuring the system searches all recent orders to find matching pending items and includes each in the per-item/net calculation and confirmation step.

actions.old_content: [acti-009] IF the user asks to set their default address to an address that already appears on a recent order and declines to re-enter it (e.g., 'use the address from my recent order') THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for the referenced order to retrieve the address and then call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
actions.new_content: acti-009: IF the user asks to set their default address to an address that already appears on a recent order and declines to re-enter it (e.g., 'use the address from my recent order') THEN: (1) call get_order_details(order_id) for the referenced recent order to retrieve the address; (2) confirm with the user which order/address they mean (explicit confirmation required) and whether they want that address applied only to the referenced pending order or also saved as their account default; (3) IF the user wants to use the address for a pending order, call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip); (4) IF the user also wants to save it as their default, call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) after confirming; (5) do not assume an address appearing on an order is already in the user's stored profile address list — always confirm source and intent before modifying either the order or the account.

actions.content: prec-010: DO NOT request, collect, or accept full raw payment card details (card number, CVV, expiry) or other sensitive payment credentials in chat. If a user attempts to provide such details, immediately inform them that for security reasons you cannot process or store that information, instruct them to add the payment method through the service's secure payment portal or app, and provide step-by-step guidance for doing so. If a payment is required to complete an action, offer to (a) charge an existing saved payment method on the account (with explicit user confirmation) or (b) wait for the user to add a new method via the secure portal before proceeding.

acti-014: IF an exchange/modify/cancellation action fails (or cannot be executed) because the selected payment method (for example a gift card) has insufficient funds to cover required charges or price differences THEN: (1) calculate the exact shortfall after applying the available balance on the chosen payment source and present the per-item and total shortfall to the user; (2) Offer to charge the remaining shortfall to another existing payment method on the account (listing available saved methods) and require explicit user confirmation before proceeding; (3) IF no other saved payment method exists or the user prefers to use a different card, instruct the user to add the new payment method via the secure payment portal (do NOT request raw card data in chat) and offer to proceed once the user confirms the new method is saved; (4) After the user confirms which payment method(s) to use, retry the action including the appropriate payment_method_id(s) and document the final charges/refunds; (5) If the system does not support split payments across multiple sources, inform the user and offer alternatives (adjust replacement choices, cancel the action, or add sufficient funds to the gift card) before retrying.

actions.old_content: IF the user states final choices for exchanges/modifications THEN: - IF the user also issues an explicit instruction to proceed (e.g., 'please do this', 'add this to my order', 'go ahead and replace it with X'), treat that as confirmation and proceed with the action tool call after restating the exact changes. - OTHERWISE, restate the exact changes (items being replaced, selected replacement variants, and price differences) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing the change.
actions.new_content: IF the user states final choices for exchanges/modifications/returns/cancellations THEN: - IF the user also issues an explicit instruction to proceed (e.g., 'please do this', 'go ahead and replace it with X', 'start the return') treat that as confirmation and proceed with the appropriate action tool call after restating the exact changes, affected order_id(s), item_id(s), and the refund/charge amounts and payment destinations. - OTHERWISE, restate the exact changes (items being replaced/removed/returned/canceled, selected replacement variants, order_id(s) involved, and price differences or refund amounts) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing the change. BEFORE executing any return or cancellation action, verify via get_order_details that each referenced item_id belongs to the target order_id and confirm that order_id with the user; DO NOT call a return/cancel tool if an item_id is not present in the target order—first resolve the correct order or item identification with the user.

actions.content: IF the user provides a user_id (for example their account handle or internal id) THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the account profile, saved addresses, payment methods, and recent orders before proceeding with order-scoped or account-scoped actions.
